mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference between falling and rising rate periods (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and drag equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE spikes by >0.5pp, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over any 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising real savings rates indicates robust economic momentum backed by household liquidity, which fuels equity risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative predictive value below 0.45 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts act as a leading indicator for construction activity and household wealth, but only when real interest rates are falling; this combination accelerates equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is stable or rising, and the regime-specific IC drops below 0.02 over a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in retail and food services sales below the 12-month moving average signals weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower during RSAFS contraction phases compared to expansion phases (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate falls below 48% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding business activity and wage-driven consumption, which systematically supports equity earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no monotonic relationship with PAYEMS growth rates, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.04 across all 2005-2024 periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces the growth of the federal funds rate, it indicates that supply-side momentum is strong enough to offset tighter financial conditions, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO growth and FEDFUNDS changes fails to predict returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing, or directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals an overheating economy that triggers aggressive Federal Reserve policy responses, which historically compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where GDP growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during post-2020 policy regimes.
